scope.question: Can machine-learning-style optimization identify trustworthy finite-central-charge Virasoro modular-bootstrap solutions in the window 1 < c <= 8/7 and sharpen the near-c=1 spectral-gap story without overclaiming CFT existence?
in_scope: Track the finite-c Virasoro modular-bootstrap benchmark literature from 2013 through 2026
Compare classic SDP, fast truncation, integrality, geometric, and ML-optimized modular-bootstrap constraints in a single convention set
Treat finite c as finite central charge and keep the torus-partition-function problem explicitly 2d and Virasoro based
Define evidence standards for candidate spectra, truncation uncertainty, and gap-bound improvement claims
out_of_scope: Claiming a rigorous proof that the ML-generated candidate spectra correspond to exact new CFTs
Exploring non-Virasoro chiral algebras, mixed-correlator crossing systems, or large-c holographic limits as the primary target
Running a full production numerical scan or SDPB campaign in this workspace during the initialization pass
unresolved_questions: Which diagnostics best separate genuine nearby CFT solutions from optimizer artifacts in the 1 < c <= 8/7 window?
How much of the near-c=1 gap improvement survives once truncation and integrality systematics are combined?
Can the continuous family suggested by the 2026 ML paper be constrained further by analytic or geometric bootstrap input?

context_gaps: No local numerical pipeline or reproduced spectra are present yet
No decisive benchmark figure has been regenerated inside this workspace yet
crucial_inputs: Virasoro torus partition function modular invariance
Integrality and geometric constraints as non-ML cross-checks

formulations: Virasoro modular bootstrap for unitary compact 2d CFTs with c > 1
Finite-c benchmark comparison centered on 1 < c <= 8/7
ML-style loss minimization treated as a search heuristic, not as proof by itself
allowed_estimator_families: loss-minimization over truncated spectra with explicit uncertainty estimates
forbidden_estimator_families: qualitative candidate-spectrum matches without modular-residual or truncation diagnostics
claims of new CFT existence based only on low loss at one truncation depth
comparisons that mix inconsistent conventions for central charge, gap definition, or partition-function normalization
allowed_fit_families: benchmark-by-benchmark comparison against published gap curves and candidate spectra
uncertainty-aware local interpolation in c when reproducing published scans
forbidden_fit_families: smooth extrapolations beyond the validated c window without source support
fits chosen only for visual agreement with no literature anchor
stop_and_rethink_conditions: A result appears stronger than arXiv:2604.01275 but rests on weaker truncation control than that paper reports
A claimed finite-c improvement disappears after aligning conventions with arXiv:1608.06241 or arXiv:2308.08725
Candidate spectra look plausible but fail integrality or geometric consistency checks

observables.name: Finite-c primary gap curve
observables.definition: Upper bound or candidate value for the lowest nontrivial primary dimension Delta_gap as a function of central charge c in the finite-c Virasoro modular bootstrap window

observables.name: Candidate truncated primary spectrum
observables.definition: Low-lying primary dimensions and degeneracies entering truncated torus partition functions consistent with modular-invariance diagnostics

observables.name: Optimization and truncation diagnostic
observables.definition: Loss, modular residual, or uncertainty proxy used to judge whether an ML-optimized candidate spectrum is trustworthy

claims.statement: The project will compare whether ML-optimized modular-bootstrap searches at finite central charge reproduce or sharpen the established finite-c Virasoro gap narrative without treating low-loss candidate spectra as established new CFTs.

deliverables.description: Literature synthesis tracking the finite-c modular-bootstrap benchmark chain from 2013 through 2026
Comparison note for published finite-c gap bounds, candidate spectra, and the 1 < c <= 8/7 window
Checklist of diagnostics required before any ML-generated candidate spectrum is treated as physically meaningful

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare the finite-c gap narrative against arXiv:1608.06241, arXiv:2308.08725, arXiv:2308.11692, and arXiv:2604.01275 using a single convention set
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Every reported benchmark statement is traceable to a cited finite-c source and no benchmark formula is quoted with mismatched conventions

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that every ML-style candidate-spectrum claim is paired with an explicit truncation or residual diagnostic and at least one non-ML cross-check route
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No candidate-spectrum statement appears without uncertainty language and a named cross-check path

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review the report text for any wording that upgrades candidate solutions to established exact CFTs without decisive proof
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All speculative statements remain explicitly labeled as candidate, suggestive, or unverified

references.locator: Friedan and Keller, Constraints on 2d CFT partition functions, arXiv:1307.6562
references.why_it_matters: Foundational linear-functional finite-gap constraints for 2d modular bootstrap

references.locator: Collier, Lin, and Yin, Modular Bootstrap Revisited, arXiv:1608.06241
references.why_it_matters: Canonical finite-c Virasoro benchmark for gap bounds and extremal spectra

references.locator: Afkhami-Jeddi, Hartman, and Tajdini, Fast Conformal Bootstrap and Constraints on 3d Gravity, arXiv:1903.06272
references.why_it_matters: Supplies the fast truncated-polynomial viewpoint and high-precision modular-bootstrap numerics

references.locator: Fitzpatrick and Li, Improving Modular Bootstrap Bounds with Integrality, arXiv:2308.08725
references.why_it_matters: Demonstrates finite-c improvements from integrality, especially near c=1

references.locator: Chiang et al., The Geometry of the Modular Bootstrap, arXiv:2308.11692
references.why_it_matters: Adds the geometric and Hankel-SDP perspective plus low-order integrality structure for the modular bootstrap

references.locator: Benjamin, Fitzpatrick, Li, and Thaler, Descending into the Modular Bootstrap, arXiv:2604.01275
references.why_it_matters: Latest arXiv anchor for ML-optimized modular-bootstrap searches and the 1 < c <= 8/7 candidate-spectrum window

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Reporting a low optimization loss at one truncation order as evidence for an exact new CFT
forbidden_proxies.reason: This skips truncation uncertainty, integrality, and benchmark comparison

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Using large-c holographic intuition as the decisive argument for the finite-c window near c=1
forbidden_proxies.reason: The project scope is the finite-c Virasoro regime, not asymptotic holography

weakest_anchors: Whether low-loss candidate spectra in 1 < c <= 8/7 correspond to exact unitary compact CFTs rather than truncation artifacts
How strong the near-c=1 gap improvement really is after combining ML heuristics with non-ML constraints
unvalidated_assumptions: That the latest ML search diagnostics are strong enough to justify a stronger finite-c gap narrative
That central-charge notation c cannot drift into an unrelated speed-of-light convention later in the project
competing_explanations: The continuous family reported in arXiv:2604.01275 could reflect optimizer access to a broad near-solution manifold rather than genuine exact CFT data
Integrality or geometric constraints might explain the apparent improvement without requiring a qualitatively new ML discovery
disconfirming_observations: A convention-aligned recheck shows that the claimed near-c=1 improvement is already contained in earlier non-ML work
Candidate spectra cease to look viable once truncation uncertainty or integrality is enforced more strictly
